figures.name: Lalibela
figures.title: Architect of the New Jerusalem
figures.quote: Commanded by God in a vision to build a 'New Jerusalem' in Ethiopia.
figures.summary: The most famous king of the Zagwe Dynasty, who oversaw the excavation of eleven breathtaking monolithic rock-hewn churches from the volcanic mountains of Lasta.

figures.name: Yekuno Amlak
figures.title: Restorer of the Solomonic Line
figures.quote: The nobleman from Amhara who overthrew the Zagwe.
figures.summary: Leader of the 1270 AD rebellion that ended the Zagwe Dynasty, shifting power to the Amhara and founding the Solomonic line of kings based on the Kebra Nagast.

figures.name: Amda Seyon I
figures.title: The Lion of Judah
figures.quote: He defended Christendom against overwhelming odds.
figures.summary: A brilliant military commander who decisively defeated the Ifat Sultanate, expanding Ethiopia's borders and centralizing royal power.

figures.name: Abba Tekle Haymanot
figures.title: Founder of Debre Libanos
figures.quote: He prayed on one leg for seven years until the other withered.
figures.summary: Revered saint who founded the great Debre Libanos monastery, a spiritual powerhouse and center of learning that sustained the kingdom.

figures.name: Zara Yaqob
figures.title: Theologian-Emperor
figures.quote: Wrote the Book of Light and established the Meskal festival.
figures.summary: A formidable intellectual and military leader who reformed the Ethiopian Church, settled the Sabbath controversy, and engaged in diplomacy with Europe.

figures.name: Ahmad Gran
figures.title: The Left-Handed Jihadist
figures.quote: A military leader of Adal whose campaigns nearly destroyed the Christian kingdom.
figures.summary: Formidable general of the Sultanate of Adal who utilized Ottoman firearms to devastate the Ethiopian highlands, marking the end of medieval isolation.
